% This is generated by ESQL's AbstractFunctionTestCase. Do not edit it. See ../README.md for how to regenerate it.

## Examples

Group `@timestamp` into one-hour buckets when the request includes a `@timestamp` filter:

```esql
FROM sample_data
| STATS min = MIN(@timestamp), max = MAX(@timestamp) BY bucket = TSTEP(1 hour)
| SORT bucket
```

| min:datetime | max:datetime | bucket:datetime |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 2023-10-23T12:15:03.360Z | 2023-10-23T12:27:28.948Z | 2023-10-23T13:15:00.000Z |
| 2023-10-23T13:33:34.937Z | 2023-10-23T13:55:01.543Z | 2023-10-23T14:15:00.000Z |


The returned `bucket` values are the end timestamps of each bucket.
Boundaries are generated as `range_start + n * step` (UTC), and each bucket
represents `(bucket_end - step, bucket_end]`.

The same query with the step passed as a string literal:

```esql
FROM sample_data
| STATS min = MIN(@timestamp), max = MAX(@timestamp) BY bucket = TSTEP("1 hour")
| SORT bucket
```

| min:datetime | max:datetime | bucket:datetime |
| --- | --- | --- |
| 2023-10-23T12:15:03.360Z | 2023-10-23T12:27:28.948Z | 2023-10-23T13:15:00.000Z |
| 2023-10-23T13:33:34.937Z | 2023-10-23T13:55:01.543Z | 2023-10-23T14:15:00.000Z |
